mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation accelerates above 8% year-over-year, creating negative real rates that erode equity valuations. This regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors demand higher risk premiums and rotate away from equities.
evidence_refs: fact:As of March 2022, FEDFUNDS=0.4% and CPIAUCSL=239.828, indicating negative real rates amid inflation surge
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL > 238, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate below 5% combined with payroll growth decelerating below 100k/month signals labor market tightness without wage-driven demand, indicating Fed will accelerate rate hikes. Equity markets decline in anticipation of restrictive monetary policy over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=4.9% and PAYEMS=144598k as of February 2022
regime_hint: tight labor market with Fed tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the correlation between UNRATE < 5% AND PAYEMS deceleration and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline by more than 15% month-over-month, signaling weakening consumer demand and forward-looking recession risk. This demand shock produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following HOUST declines > 15% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines for two consecutive months while remaining above 104, indicating supply-side weakness without demand collapse. This signals stagflationary pressure that compresses equity margins, producing negative forward returns over 5 trading days as investors react to earnings revision risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS adjusted for inflation via CPIAUCSL) decline month-over-month while unemployment remains below 5%, indicating consumer demand destruction despite tight labor market. This demand-supply disconnect signals recession risk and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: demand collapse with labor market lag
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of RSAFS decline AND UNRATE < 5% does not produce significantly negative forward returns (interaction p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains below 1% while core inflation (proxied by CPIAUCSL momentum) accelerates above 7% year-over-year, creating a policy error regime. Equity markets decline as investors anticipate aggressive Fed tightening and terminal rate repricing over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.4% and CPIAUCSL=239.828 as of March 2022, indicating policy lag
regime_hint: monetary policy error with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% AND CPIAUCSL momentum > 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth remains above 140k/month while unemployment rate stays below 5%, indicating sustained labor demand. This tight labor market without supply response produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors interpret strong employment as earnings support, offsetting Fed tightening concerns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=144598k and UNRATE=4.9% as of February 2022
regime_hint: tight labor market with earnings support
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window, or 5-day forward returns during periods of PAYEMS > 140k AND UNRATE < 5% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains above 2% annualized while housing starts decline below 1200k units, indicating demand rotation away from housing toward services. This sectoral shift produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reweight toward non-housing beneficiaries and growth expectations stabilize.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=16570.2B and HOUST=1211.0k as of Q4 2021 and January 2022
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when GDPC1 > 16500 AND HOUST < 1200k, or the interaction effect between GDP growth and housing decline is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
